commands.review_summary:
The PR rediscovers the current xAI token endpoint when a persisted OAuth credential still points at the retired `https://auth.x.ai/oauth/token`, then adds a regression test for that refresh path.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +41. Total +61 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level and dependency-contract path: current main posts refresh to the cached endpoint, and live xAI probes show the retired endpoint returns HTML while discovery points to `/oauth2/token`. A full real stale-token OpenClaw refresh was not run in this review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The patch has no narrow code repair to queue, but it needs contributor proof or maintainer override before merge.

target.title: fix(xai): rediscover retired OAuth token endpoint
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Some persisted xAI OAuth credentials can carry the retired token endpoint `https://auth.x.ai/oauth/token`. Current xAI discovery returns `https://auth.x.ai/oauth2/token`, but refresh previously trusted the cached credential endpoint first, so those stale credentials posted refresh requests to the retired endpoint and received Cloudflare HTML instead of OAuth JSON.

## Why This Change Was Made

`extensions/xai/xai-oauth.ts` already stores the discovered endpoint for new OAuth and device-code credentials. This change only special-cases the known retired xAI endpoint during refresh: if a credential has `/oauth/token`, OpenClaw rediscovers the current token endpoint before posting, and the successful refresh writes the canonical `/oauth2/token` endpoint back into the credential.

Minimal live repro with fake tokens:

```bash
for endpoint in "https://auth.x.ai/oauth2/token" "https://auth.x.ai/oauth/token"; do
  echo "endpoint=$endpoint"
  curl -sS -X POST "$endpoint" \
    -H "Accept: application/json" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
    --data "grant_type=refresh_token&client_id=b1a00492-073a-47ea-816f-4c329264a828&refresh_token=not-a-real-refresh-token" \
    -w "\nhttp_code=%{http_code} content_type=%{content_type} server=%header{server}\n"
done
```

Observed live result while preparing this PR:

```text
https://auth.x.ai/oauth2/token -> 400 application/json, OAuth error invalid_grant
https://auth.x.ai/oauth/token  -> 403 text/html; charset=UTF-8, server cloudflare
```

## User Impact

Users with stale persisted xAI OAuth credentials no longer get stuck on the retired Cloudflare-served endpoint. Their next refresh attempts the current endpoint from xAI discovery and normalizes the stored endpoint after a successful refresh.

## Evidence

- `pnpm test extensions/xai/xai-oauth.test.ts`
- Live dummy POST probe above confirmed `/oauth2/token` returns OAuth JSON while `/oauth/token` returns Cloudflare HTML.
target.author: jaaneek
